# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

require "base64"

module ActionDispatch
  module Session
    class MarshalCookieStore < CookieStore
      DEFAULT_COOKIE_KEY = "_session".freeze

      def initialize(app, options = {})
        options = options.dup
        options[:key] ||= DEFAULT_COOKIE_KEY
        super
      end

      private

      def load_session(req)
        sid = nil
        data = unpack_marshaled_cookie(req)
        [sid, data]
      end

      def write_session(req, sid, session_data, options)
        cookies = cookie_jar(req)
        payload = Base64.strict_encode64(Marshal.dump(session_data))
        cookies[options[:key]] = payload
        sid
      end

      def delete_session(req, sid, options)
        cookie_jar(req).delete(options[:key], options)
        generate_sid
      end

      def unpack_marshaled_cookie(req)
        cookies = cookie_jar(req)
        raw = cookies[@key]
        return {} unless raw && raw.bytesize > 0
        Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(raw))
      rescue
        {}
      end

      def cookie_jar(req)
        req.cookie_jar
      end
    end
  end
end

Rails.application.config.session_store :marshal_cookie_store, key: "_session", secure: Rails.env.production?, httponly: true
